nameMeanings.name: Conley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Down, Antrim, and Louth): shortened form of   Connolly  .

variants.name: Connolly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, Bridie, Seamus, Aidan, Dermot, Eamon, Patrick Joseph, Colm, Cormac, Declan, Eoin.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Conghaile ‘descendant of Conghal’, a name meaning ‘hound valiant’, or of Ó Conghalaigh ‘descendant of Conghalach’, a derivative of Conghal; the two surnames have long been confused. Anglicized Irish forms Conneely, Connealy, Cunneely have arisen where loss of gh has caused lengthening of the second syllable of Conghal: they often also (Connacht) represent shortened forms of McNeilly from Mac Conghaile. Another possible origin is the West Cork name Mac Coingheallaigh (or Ó Coingheallaigh) ‘son (or descendant) of Coingheallach’, a personal name meaning ‘faithful to pledges’. See McNeilly .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1716: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

Historical Boundaries:  1623: Yorkshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America  1664: Maine, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America  1668: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America  1680: Massachusetts Claim, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America  1691: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America  1776: York, Massachusetts, United States  1820: York, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Conley was born in 1598, in Tiverton, Devon, England, her father, Abraham Conley, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth, was 20. She had at least 1 daughter with James Luke Heard. She died on 3 July 1692, in Kittery, York, Maine, United States, at the age of 94.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Conley(이)가 1598년 영국 데폰 티버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Abraham Conley(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 James Luke Heard(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1692년 7월 3일 미국 메인 주 요르크 키터이에서 94세의 나이로 사망했다.
